methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Dragonfly Aerospace is a Series G-stage company in Space & Aerospace founded by Bryan Dean, having raised $20M to date. The sector comprises 6 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and a broader private peer set including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Stage-progression valuation is the applicable method here. Using total raised ($20M) as a proxy for enterprise value and applying a typical Series G-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.25x yields an implied valuation of $51M. This method is grounded in observed financing progression patterns at this maturity level. Peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Space & Aerospace set; while 23 same-sector peers exist, filtering for stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 49.8x P/S median across five public tickers cannot be applied without the target's disclosed revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $44M to $59M, centered on $51M. This single-method result carries elevated uncertainty given reliance on stage-progression mechanics alone. Key risks include the absence of direct peer valuation support and unknown revenue metrics that would enable public-comp validation. Any update to disclosed financials or identification of additional same-stage private comparables would materially sharpen this range.
